2604.13519.title: ToolSpec: Accelerating Tool Calling via Schema-Aware and Retrieval-Augmented Speculative Decoding
2604.13519.paper_summary: Tool calling has greatly expanded the practical utility of large language models (LLMs) by enabling them to interact with external applications. As LLM capabilities advance, effective tool use increasingly involves multi-step, multi-turn interactions to solve complex tasks. However, the resulting growth in tool interactions incurs substantial latency, posing a key challenge for real-time LLM serving. Through empirical analysis, we find that tool-calling traces are highly structured, conform to constrained schemas, and often exhibit recurring invocation patterns. Motivated by this, we propose ToolSpec, a schema-aware, retrieval-augmented speculative decoding method for accelerating tool calling. ToolSpec exploits predefined tool schemas to generate accurate drafts, using a finite-state machine to alternate between deterministic schema token filling and speculative generation for variable fields. In addition, ToolSpec retrieves similar historical tool invocations and reuses them as drafts to further improve efficiency. ToolSpec presents a plug-and-play solution that can be seamlessly integrated into existing LLM workflows. Experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that ToolSpec achieves up to a 4.2x speedup, substantially outperforming existing training-free speculative decoding methods.
2604.13519.paper_summary_zh: 工具調用大大擴展了大型語言模型 (LLMs) 的實用性，使其能夠與外部應用程序互動。隨著 LLM 能力的提升，有效的工具使用越來越涉及多步驟、多輪次的互動，以解決複雜任務。然而，隨之而來的工具互動增長會產生可觀的延遲，這對實時 LLM 服務構成了主要挑戰。通過實證分析，我們發現工具調用痕跡高度結構化，符合受限的架構，並且經常顯示出重複的調用模式。基於此，我們提出了 ToolSpec，一種具有架構感知的檢索增強推測解碼方法，用於加速工具調用。ToolSpec 利用預定義的工具架構生成準確的草稿，使用有限狀態機在確定性架構標記填充和可變字段的推測生成之間切換。此外，ToolSpec 檢索類似的歷史工具調用並將其重用作草稿，以進一步提高效率。ToolSpec 提供了一個即插即用的解決方案，可以無縫集成到現有的 LLM 工作流程中。多個基準上的實驗表明，ToolSpec 實現了高達 4.2 倍的加速，顯著超越了現有的無需訓練的推測解碼方法。
2604.13519.author: Heming Xia et.al.
2604.13519.authors: Heming Xia, Yongqi Li, Cunxiao Du, Mingbo Song, Wenjie Li
